meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no version control (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no version control. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kits to Solve Version Control
content.seo_description: Stop losing code. Learn how a SaaS starter kit solves version control issues for freelancers using Antigravity and Claude Code. Build faster today.
content.answer_block: Stop manually tracking file changes. By deploying a standardized SaaS starter kit, you enforce version control at the architectural level. Using Antigravity to scaffold and Claude Code to manage iterative commits, you transform chaotic development into a repeatable, audit-ready workflow that keeps your client projects professional and secure.
content.intro:
You know the sinking feeling: a client asks for a rollback on a feature you shipped three days ago, but your local folder is a graveyard of 'final_v2_updated' files. You aren't just losing time; you are losing professional credibility. The lack of robust version control isn't just a technical oversight—it is a business liability that threatens your reputation as a high-end freelancer.

But what if your development environment had a 'memory' that worked as hard as you do? By shifting to a structured SaaS starter kit, you move away from the chaos of fragmented code and into a lifecycle-managed ecosystem. This approach doesn't just save your work; it allows you to ship complex features with the surgical precision of a senior engineer.

steps.title: Initialize the Starter Kit Scaffold
steps.description: Establish a clean, versioned baseline using Antigravity to ensure all future code commits have a consistent structure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready SaaS boilerplate with integrated Git hooks, Husky for commit linting, and a standardized folder structure for a Next.js/Supabase stack.

steps.title: Configure Atomic Commit Protocols
steps.description: Automate your versioning strategy so that every incremental change is documented and reversible.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a .husky/pre-commit configuration that enforces Conventional Commits and runs type-checking before any code is staged.

steps.title: Implement Claude Code Orchestration
steps.description: Connect your development environment to Claude Code to manage complex refactors while maintaining strict version integrity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a .claudeignore file that prevents build artifacts and local environment secrets from polluting the version history.

steps.title: Automate Feature Branching Workflows
steps.description: Ensure every client request is isolated in a feature branch, protecting the main production codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a script that automates the creation of feature branches, linking them to a predefined tracking ID format for client reporting.

steps.title: Verify Audit Trails
steps.description: Perform a final validation to ensure all changes are tracked and the environment is ready for client handoff.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a summary report script that lists the last 10 commits with their associated architectural changes for client documentation.

pro_tips: Always use a 'semantic versioning' tag for every client delivery; it builds trust and makes rollbacks trivial.
Use Claude Code to generate your changelogs automatically from your commit history to save hours of manual documentation.
Keep your .env files out of your repo, but include a .env.example with placeholders so clients can get started instantly.
Implement 'Branch Protection Rules' on GitHub even for small projects; it prevents accidental force-pushes that destroy history.
Use Antigravity to script a 'reset-to-production' command that wipes local testing data without destroying your source code.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is version-controlled, focus on setting up a CI/CD pipeline that automatically deploys to a staging environment on every pull request.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools to stop the chaos and start shipping like a pro. Don't let another project end in a 'final_v3' nightmare. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
